title: submit-speculate output data
description:
Result of ``submit-speculate``.

``speculated`` is False on the budget-dedup no-op path (a validated-fresh
canary already exists for this ``(cmd_sha, version)`` — S2 will reuse it) and
True when a fresh speculative canary fired. ``verified`` mirrors the canary
verification outcome. This is NOT a sequenced block, so it carries no
``next_block`` — S1's brief owns the next-block suggestion.

run_id.description: The (canary) run this speculation operated on.
run_id.title: Run Id

speculated.description: True when a fresh speculative canary fired; False on the budget no-op (a validated-fresh canary already exists — S2 will reuse it).
speculated.title: Speculated

verified.description: True when the speculative canary verified (or a fresh one is already validated).
verified.title: Verified

canary_run_id.description: The speculative canary's run_id, when one fired.
canary_run_id.title: Canary Run Id

canary_job_ids.description: The speculative canary's scheduler ids, when one fired.
canary_job_ids.title: Canary Job Ids

failure_kind.description: The verify failure kind when the speculative canary failed.
failure_kind.title: Failure Kind

started.description: Detach-by-contract handle (design §3): True when the speculative canary was handed to a durable detached worker and this call returned immediately. ``verified`` is not yet known (the poll runs in the worker) — read the outcome from the journal. False on the synchronous path and on the budget no-op.
started.title: Started

watch.description: How to learn the detached canary's outcome — ``"journal"`` when ``started`` is True. None otherwise.
watch.title: Watch

detached_pid.description: The detached worker's OS process id (informational). None otherwise.
detached_pid.title: Detached Pid
